attributes.content:
## Overview

The Checklist card gives the workspace a structured home for simple ordered list of check items. Lighter weight than a playbook - no agent tracking, just checkboxes.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Checklist is designed to participate in active workflows. Its state, relationships, and outputs need to remain inspectable so that agents and humans can collaborate on the same object without losing context.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Task]]** -- Checklist often creates follow-up work or is itself tracked through task execution.
- **[[Project]]** -- Checklist can be grouped under a larger initiative so its work stays visible in context.
- **[[Kanban Board]]** -- Checklist can be surfaced in board views when teams need a visual operational workflow.
- **[[Workflow Playbook]]** -- Checklist can be created, reviewed, or progressed as one step inside a repeatable workflow.
- **[[Trigger]]** -- Changes to Checklist can fire automation, and automation can also create or update Checklist.
- **[[Case]]** -- Case can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Checklist.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Checklist as the structured home for simple ordered list of check items. Lighter weight than a playbook - no agent tracking, just checkboxes and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Checklist when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Checklist stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.

cardInfo.name: Checklist
cardInfo.summary: Simple ordered list of check items. Lighter weight than a playbook - no agent tracking, just checkboxes.
